mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques like PagedAttention and continuous batching. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the vLLM CLI tool to manage deployments and configurations.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.18.0

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster with the desired version and node configuration.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local environment to interact with the new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Deploy vLLM
steps.description:
Deploy the vLLM application to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/kubernetes/vllm-deployment.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains the deployment configuration.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment vllm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/kubernetes/vllm-monitoring.yaml

```

> This command enables autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> This YAML file sets up monitoring.

uninstall.title: Remove vLLM Deployment
uninstall.description:
This will delete the vLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that any data stored in persistent volumes may be lost.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/kubernetes/vllm-deployment.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. Drain the nodes if necessary, then perform the upgrade and verify the deployment.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl set image deployment/vllm vllm=<new-image>:<new-tag>

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you want to drain.
> Specify the new image version for vLLM.
> Bring the node back online after the upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running with the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image version may not exist or is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the image tag and ensure it is available in the container registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the vLLM deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the resources allocated to existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be properly exposed or running.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is running and check the service configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved memory management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your deployment configurations to align with the new model types.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods' showing all pods in a 'Running' state, indicating that vLLM is successfully deployed.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools installed and a suitable cloud environment set up.
